Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class LegalEntityUpdate

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.LegalEntityUpdate
public class LegalEntityUpdate extends Object
LegalEntityUpdate

A legal entity. Legal entities are organizations, such as companies, that participate in financial transactions

  • Constructor Details

    • LegalEntityUpdate

      public LegalEntityUpdate(Optional<Boolean> accreditedInvestor, Optional<Boolean> adviser, Optional<Boolean> brokerDealer, Optional<? extends LegalEntityUpdateBusinessIndustrialClassification> businessIndustrialClassification, Optional<? extends LegalEntityUpdateCorporateStructure> corporateStructure, Optional<String> correspondentId, Optional<? extends List<String>> doingBusinessAs, Optional<? extends EntityDueDiligenceUpdate> entityDueDiligence, Optional<String> entityName, Optional<? extends LegalEntityUpdateEntityType> entityType, Optional<? extends LegalEntityUpdateExemptCustomerReason> exemptCustomerReason, Optional<Boolean> exemptVerifyingBeneficialOwners, Optional<String> forTheBenefitOf, Optional<Boolean> foreignFinancialInstitution, Optional<? extends DateUpdate> formationDate, Optional<Boolean> institutionalCustomer, Optional<? extends LargeTraderUpdate> largeTrader, Optional<? extends PostalAddressUpdate> legalAddress, Optional<String> leiCode, Optional<? extends List<String>> operatingRegions, Optional<String> registrationRegion, Optional<Boolean> regulatedInvestmentCompany, Optional<? extends List<String>> relatedDocumentIds, Optional<Boolean> revocableTrust, Optional<Boolean> subjectToBackupWithholding, Optional<String> taxId, Optional<? extends LegalEntityUpdateTaxIdType> taxIdType, Optional<? extends TaxProfileUpdate> taxProfile)

    • LegalEntityUpdate

      public LegalEntityUpdate()

  • Method Details

    • accreditedInvestor

      public Optional<Boolean> accreditedInvestor()
      Indicates whether the entity is an accredited investor. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • adviser

      public Optional<Boolean> adviser()
      Indicates whether the entity is an adviser. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • brokerDealer

      public Optional<Boolean> brokerDealer()
      Indicates whether the entity is a broker dealer. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • businessIndustrialClassification

      public Optional<LegalEntityUpdateBusinessIndustrialClassification> businessIndustrialClassification()

    • corporateStructure

      public Optional<LegalEntityUpdateCorporateStructure> corporateStructure()
      Corporate structure of the entity.

    • correspondentId

      public Optional<String> correspondentId()
      The correspondent id associated with the legal entity.

    • doingBusinessAs

      public Optional<List<String>> doingBusinessAs()
      DBA (Doing Business As) names. Can list up to 5 associated with the Legal Entity

    • entityDueDiligence

      public Optional<EntityDueDiligenceUpdate> entityDueDiligence()
      Due Diligence for Legal Entities required when a Legal Entity is the Primary Owner on an Account.

    • entityName

      public Optional<String> entityName()
      The legal entity name.

    • entityType

      public Optional<LegalEntityUpdateEntityType> entityType()
      The entity type.

    • exemptCustomerReason

      public Optional<LegalEntityUpdateExemptCustomerReason> exemptCustomerReason()
      **Field Dependencies:**

      Exempt entities must set `exempt_verifying_beneficial_owners` to `true` and provide an `exempt_customer_reason` on the owner record.

      Required if `exempt_verifying_beneficial_owners` is `true`.

      Otherwise, must be empty.

    • exemptVerifyingBeneficialOwners

      public Optional<Boolean> exemptVerifyingBeneficialOwners()
      Indicates whether the entity is exempt from verifying beneficial owners and Enhanced Due Diligence. By default, this is set to `false`

    • forTheBenefitOf

      public Optional<String> forTheBenefitOf()
      If the legal entity is a trust, they may set this field to convey ownership and value to a trustee.

    • foreignFinancialInstitution

      public Optional<Boolean> foreignFinancialInstitution()
      Indicates whether the entity is a foreign financial institution. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • formationDate

      public Optional<DateUpdate> formationDate()
      Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following:

      * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values * A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary * A year on its own, with zero month and day values * A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration date

      Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

    • institutionalCustomer

      public Optional<Boolean> institutionalCustomer()
      Indicates whether the entity is an institutional customer

    • largeTrader

      public Optional<LargeTraderUpdate> largeTrader()
      A large trader.

    • legalAddress

      public Optional<PostalAddressUpdate> legalAddress()
      Represents a postal address, e.g. for postal delivery or payments addresses. Given a postal address, a postal service can deliver items to a premise, P.O. Box or similar. It is not intended to model geographical locations (roads, towns, mountains).

      In typical usage an address would be created via user input or from importing existing data, depending on the type of process.

      Advice on address input / editing: - Use an i18n-ready address widget such as https://github.com/google/libaddressinput) - Users should not be presented with UI elements for input or editing of fields outside countries where that field is used.

      For more guidance on how to use this schema, please see: https://support.google.com/business/answer/6397478

    • leiCode

      public Optional<String> leiCode()
      The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is the financial industry term for a unique global identifier for legal entities participating in financial transactions

    • operatingRegions

      public Optional<List<String>> operatingRegions()
      The operational footprint of an entity. Operating regions encompass all countries and regions where a company has a significant business presence This includes locations with physical offices, manufacturing plants, service centers, and sales and marketing activities Regions must be provided as two-character CLDR country codes

    • registrationRegion

      public Optional<String> registrationRegion()
      The legal home of an entity. A region of registration, in the context of a corporation, refers to the specific geographic area where the corporation is legally registered and incorporated Defines the legal jurisdiction and framework under which the corporation operates, including legal regulations, tax obligations, and compliance requirements Region must be provided as a two-character CLDR country code

    • regulatedInvestmentCompany

      public Optional<Boolean> regulatedInvestmentCompany()
      Indicates whether the entity is a regulated investment company. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • relatedDocumentIds

      public Optional<List<String>> relatedDocumentIds()
      Document ids related to the legal entity. At least one is required for RIA correspondents when creating Estate or Trust accounts.

    • revocableTrust

      public Optional<Boolean> revocableTrust()
      Indicates whether the trust is a revocable trust. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • subjectToBackupWithholding

      public Optional<Boolean> subjectToBackupWithholding()
      Boolean indicator whether the LE is subject to backup withholding

    • taxId

      public Optional<String> taxId()
      The full U.S. tax ID for a related entity; Must be provided with `EIN` tax ID type

    • taxIdType

      public Optional<LegalEntityUpdateTaxIdType> taxIdType()
      The nature of the U.S. Tax ID indicated in the related tax_id field; Examples include ITIN, SSN, EIN.

    • taxProfile

      public Optional<TaxProfileUpdate> taxProfile()
      Tax Profile pertaining to the Legal Entity or Natural Person.

    • withExemptCustomerReason

      public LegalEntityUpdate withExemptCustomerReason(LegalEntityUpdateExemptCustomerReason exemptCustomerReason)
      **Field Dependencies:**

      Exempt entities must set `exempt_verifying_beneficial_owners` to `true` and provide an `exempt_customer_reason` on the owner record.

      Required if `exempt_verifying_beneficial_owners` is `true`.

      Otherwise, must be empty.

    • withExemptCustomerReason

      public LegalEntityUpdate withExemptCustomerReason(Optional<? extends LegalEntityUpdateExemptCustomerReason> exemptCustomerReason)
      **Field Dependencies:**

      Exempt entities must set `exempt_verifying_beneficial_owners` to `true` and provide an `exempt_customer_reason` on the owner record.

      Required if `exempt_verifying_beneficial_owners` is `true`.

      Otherwise, must be empty.

      Indicates whether the entity is exempt from verifying beneficial owners and Enhanced Due Diligence. By default, this is set to `false`

      Indicates whether the entity is exempt from verifying beneficial owners and Enhanced Due Diligence. By default, this is set to `false`

    • withFormationDate

      public LegalEntityUpdate withFormationDate(DateUpdate formationDate)
      Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following:

      * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values * A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary * A year on its own, with zero month and day values * A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration date

      Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

    • withFormationDate

      public LegalEntityUpdate withFormationDate(Optional<? extends DateUpdate> formationDate)
      Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following:

      * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values * A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary * A year on its own, with zero month and day values * A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration date

      Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

    • withLegalAddress

      public LegalEntityUpdate withLegalAddress(PostalAddressUpdate legalAddress)
      Represents a postal address, e.g. for postal delivery or payments addresses. Given a postal address, a postal service can deliver items to a premise, P.O. Box or similar. It is not intended to model geographical locations (roads, towns, mountains).

      In typical usage an address would be created via user input or from importing existing data, depending on the type of process.

      Advice on address input / editing: - Use an i18n-ready address widget such as https://github.com/google/libaddressinput) - Users should not be presented with UI elements for input or editing of fields outside countries where that field is used.

      For more guidance on how to use this schema, please see: https://support.google.com/business/answer/6397478

    • withLegalAddress

      public LegalEntityUpdate withLegalAddress(Optional<? extends PostalAddressUpdate> legalAddress)
      Represents a postal address, e.g. for postal delivery or payments addresses. Given a postal address, a postal service can deliver items to a premise, P.O. Box or similar. It is not intended to model geographical locations (roads, towns, mountains).

      In typical usage an address would be created via user input or from importing existing data, depending on the type of process.

      Advice on address input / editing: - Use an i18n-ready address widget such as https://github.com/google/libaddressinput) - Users should not be presented with UI elements for input or editing of fields outside countries where that field is used.

      For more guidance on how to use this schema, please see: https://support.google.com/business/answer/6397478

    • withLeiCode

      public LegalEntityUpdate withLeiCode(String leiCode)
      The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is the financial industry term for a unique global identifier for legal entities participating in financial transactions

    • withLeiCode

      public LegalEntityUpdate withLeiCode(Optional<String> leiCode)
      The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is the financial industry term for a unique global identifier for legal entities participating in financial transactions

    • withOperatingRegions

      public LegalEntityUpdate withOperatingRegions(List<String> operatingRegions)
      The operational footprint of an entity. Operating regions encompass all countries and regions where a company has a significant business presence This includes locations with physical offices, manufacturing plants, service centers, and sales and marketing activities Regions must be provided as two-character CLDR country codes

    • withOperatingRegions

      public LegalEntityUpdate withOperatingRegions(Optional<? extends List<String>> operatingRegions)
      The operational footprint of an entity. Operating regions encompass all countries and regions where a company has a significant business presence This includes locations with physical offices, manufacturing plants, service centers, and sales and marketing activities Regions must be provided as two-character CLDR country codes

    • withRegistrationRegion

      public LegalEntityUpdate withRegistrationRegion(String registrationRegion)
      The legal home of an entity. A region of registration, in the context of a corporation, refers to the specific geographic area where the corporation is legally registered and incorporated Defines the legal jurisdiction and framework under which the corporation operates, including legal regulations, tax obligations, and compliance requirements Region must be provided as a two-character CLDR country code

    • withRegistrationRegion

      public LegalEntityUpdate withRegistrationRegion(Optional<String> registrationRegion)
      The legal home of an entity. A region of registration, in the context of a corporation, refers to the specific geographic area where the corporation is legally registered and incorporated Defines the legal jurisdiction and framework under which the corporation operates, including legal regulations, tax obligations, and compliance requirements Region must be provided as a two-character CLDR country code

    • withRegulatedInvestmentCompany

      public LegalEntityUpdate withRegulatedInvestmentCompany(boolean regulatedInvestmentCompany)
      Indicates whether the entity is a regulated investment company. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • withRegulatedInvestmentCompany

      public LegalEntityUpdate withRegulatedInvestmentCompany(Optional<Boolean> regulatedInvestmentCompany)
      Indicates whether the entity is a regulated investment company. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • withRelatedDocumentIds

      public LegalEntityUpdate withRelatedDocumentIds(List<String> relatedDocumentIds)
      Document ids related to the legal entity. At least one is required for RIA correspondents when creating Estate or Trust accounts.

    • withRelatedDocumentIds

      public LegalEntityUpdate withRelatedDocumentIds(Optional<? extends List<String>> relatedDocumentIds)
      Document ids related to the legal entity. At least one is required for RIA correspondents when creating Estate or Trust accounts.

    • withRevocableTrust

      public LegalEntityUpdate withRevocableTrust(boolean revocableTrust)
      Indicates whether the trust is a revocable trust. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • withRevocableTrust

      public LegalEntityUpdate withRevocableTrust(Optional<Boolean> revocableTrust)
      Indicates whether the trust is a revocable trust. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • withSubjectToBackupWithholding

      public LegalEntityUpdate withSubjectToBackupWithholding(boolean subjectToBackupWithholding)
      Boolean indicator whether the LE is subject to backup withholding

    • withSubjectToBackupWithholding

      public LegalEntityUpdate withSubjectToBackupWithholding(Optional<Boolean> subjectToBackupWithholding)
      Boolean indicator whether the LE is subject to backup withholding

    • withTaxId

      public LegalEntityUpdate withTaxId(String taxId)
      The full U.S. tax ID for a related entity; Must be provided with `EIN` tax ID type

    • withTaxId

      public LegalEntityUpdate withTaxId(Optional<String> taxId)
      The full U.S. tax ID for a related entity; Must be provided with `EIN` tax ID type

    • withTaxIdType

      public LegalEntityUpdate withTaxIdType(LegalEntityUpdateTaxIdType taxIdType)
      The nature of the U.S. Tax ID indicated in the related tax_id field; Examples include ITIN, SSN, EIN.

    • withTaxIdType

      public LegalEntityUpdate withTaxIdType(Optional<? extends LegalEntityUpdateTaxIdType> taxIdType)
      The nature of the U.S. Tax ID indicated in the related tax_id field; Examples include ITIN, SSN, EIN.

    • withTaxProfile

      public LegalEntityUpdate withTaxProfile(TaxProfileUpdate taxProfile)
      Tax Profile pertaining to the Legal Entity or Natural Person.

    • withTaxProfile

      public LegalEntityUpdate withTaxProfile(Optional<? extends TaxProfileUpdate> taxProfile)
      Tax Profile pertaining to the Legal Entity or Natural Person.
